source: claude-haiku-4-5 over data/omeda/items.json only; every number ground-checked against the item, failing lines dropped

items.marksman-crest: Physical damage dealers who basic attack frequently want early lifesteal and attack speed to sustain and evolve.
items.orb-of-enlightenment: Magic users need raw power and bulk, especially when fights extend and passive health recovery matters.
items.wardens-faith: Tanking enemies stacking critical strikes want armor and health to take 20% less burst damage.
items.terminus: Physical damage dealers with healing want to convert excess recovery into shields that persist after skirmishes.
items.warrior-crest: Bruisers start here for health and omnivamp to heal during sustained fights, then evolve for more.
items.occult-crest: Magic damage dealers begin with balanced stats and healing, scaling into stronger magical crests through gameplay.
items.keeper-crest: Support heroes generate gold and mana while enabling allies, triggering charges to fund their progression.
items.time-flux-band: Ability-focused heroes need mana efficiency and escape tools, teleporting to reset cooldowns and reposition safely.
items.crystal-tear: Support and magic users amplify teammates by granting both partners 30 power and 15 ability haste.
items.enras-blessing: Protectors pair with immobilized allies, instantly shielding them for 3.5 seconds on a per-target basis.
items.gaussian-greaves: Melee fighters who need escape tools and initiation damage; the double jump and shockwave slow lock down targets.
items.lunaria: Support healers trading offense for sustained healing; charges build passively and heal the most vulnerable ally.
items.mutilator: Sustained damage dealers who want omnivamp; hitting the same target three times deals significant max health damage.
items.lifebinder: Mages who want lifesteal scaling; 80 magical power plus 12% magical lifesteal fuels endless fights.
items.storm-breaker: Attack speed carries who leverage basic attacks; momentum stacks reward consistent hitting with multi-target chain lightning.
items.dread: Bruisers fighting mixed damage; 55 power and 40 magical armor block repeated magical bursts with a 20-second refresh.
items.tempest: Free starting item for early mage pressure; deals repeating area damage while self-healing 35% of output over 7 seconds.
items.lifecore: Mage supports who heal on ability spam; triggers automatic healing wisps at 100 charges toward wounded allies.
items.rift-walkers: Free starting item for supports; adds a ward, map vision, and a cooldown dash that pulls enemies.
items.solaris: Physical attackers who want healing and burst damage with 20% crit chance and a 1.5s cooldown empowered strike.
items.onixian-quiver: Crit-focused attackers who fire extra projectiles and gain 25% total crit chance plus damage reduction when low.
items.dreambinder: Mages who need bulk and want to slow enemies by 25% for 1s with every spell cast.
items.prophecy: Hybrid attackers mixing attack speed and magic power to deal 20 bonus damage on-hit with ability cooldown reduction.
items.viper: Physical fighters stacking armor reduction up to 6 times on one target while dealing 8% more damage overall.
items.nex: Starter item: low-cost penetration and ability cooldown for gankers using a 90s cooldown that deals 100% damage.
items.flux-matrix: Mages defending against enemy magic by taking 10% more magical damage but cutting their crowd control resistance in half.
items.absolution: Tanky fighters needing 75 tenacity and a 45s cooldown granting 25% damage reduction and 40% speed for 3s.
items.tainted-blade: Physical damagers shutting down enemy healing by 45% for 3s while making targets take 5% more total damage.
items.everbloom: Supports shielding allies with 5% damage reduction and 20 tenacity for 2s while boosting heal and shield strength.
items.combustion: Mages who want to spam their magic damage ability every 15 seconds with 85 power and penetrate defenses.
items.florescence: Supports who place wards, gain an extra ward slot, and earn steady gold while regenerating 50 health per second.
items.silentium: Supports who shut down one enemy hero for 0.75 seconds every 135 seconds while gaining an extra ward and steady gold.
items.draconum: Fighters who heal themselves more during combat and gain 40 armor to stay in fights longer.
items.obelisk: Mages dealing 50 damage per attack that scales with 30 power, collecting souls to grow stronger.
items.magnify: Mages who reduce enemy magic armor by 6% stacking to 15% total, healing themselves 10% of damage dealt.
items.spear-of-desolation: Physical fighters dealing 10% more damage for 6 seconds while gaining 25 ultimate charge faster.
items.raiment-of-renewal: Tanks with 700 health who regenerate 3% of missing health every second plus 80 tenacity for crowd control resistance.
items.tranquility: Supports healing 150 damage to allies every 40 seconds while giving 10% damage reduction and gaining extra ward slots.
items.citadel: Fighters with 35 armor who reduce enemy armor by 20% in team fights while dealing 35 physical damage.
items.galaxy-greaves: Supports and junglers who need magic defense, mana sustain, and jump mobility for roaming and escaping.
items.earthshaker: Melee ability-focused heroes who benefit from stacking ability damage fast while gaining health and attack speed.
items.frosted-lure: Tanky heroes who want to control fights with a damage-dealing slow shield that resets every 45 seconds.
items.gaia-greaves: Bruisers and tanks who chain basic attacks to build movement speed stacks, then convert them into bonus damage.
items.giants-ring: Any hero wanting reliable cooldown reduction and damage mitigation that doubles every 30 seconds for burst windows.
items.reclamation: Supports who need to cleanse allies, place extra wards, and gain passive gold despite minion kill penalties.
items.overlord: Fighters and tanks whose basic attacks deal area damage that scales with physical power and stacks permanent health.
items.overseer: Magic-focused heroes who want high damage output plus healing conversion from damage dealt to enemy heroes.
items.consort-crest: Supports in early game who generate free gold and damage every 30 seconds when an ally is nearby.
items.abyssal-dart: Ranged heroes needing mobility and burst via a teleport dart that marks and slows targets for 2.5 seconds.
items.razorback: Tank item for sustained fights: boosts armor by 20% and bounces 15% of hero damage back.
items.magician-crest: Mana-hungry mage starter: restores 4 mana per ability, evolving once you hit 300 total mana.
items.omen: Physical carry item: stacks damage, cuts non-ultimate cooldowns by 25% with empowered auto attacks.
items.astral-catalyst: Mage finisher: 20% ultimate cooldown reduction and 40% bonus damage on ability hits.
items.berserkers-axe: Fighter item: empowered attacks deal 50 damage, slow enemies, and grant movement speed bursts.
items.nyr-warboots: Starting tank boots: decaying speed boost and 3% health regen per second for 5 seconds.
items.amulet-of-chaos: Ability spammer: cuts cooldowns by 65% and refunds 35% of missing mana per cast.
items.inquisition: Mage tank hybrid: tanky stats with 65 damage waves every 1.5 seconds, 45% stronger vs monsters.
items.brutallax: Sustain fighter: omnivamp healing, full debuff cleanse, and 100% tenacity refresh on successful hits.
items.nuclear-rounds: Crit carry: high attack speed and 20% crit chance, boosting ability damage by 10-20%.
items.claw-of-hermes: Mages who want to punish enemies for having large mana pools and scale their damage output simultaneously.
items.rapture: Attackers who need sustain and want to drain enemies while dealing extra on-hit damage through basic attacks.
items.spirit-of-amir: Magical damage dealers who jungle, gaining lifesteal in the jungle and bonus damage against monsters for farming.
items.truesilver-bracelet: Supports and tanks needing to survive burst damage with a shield that blocks crowd control for 4 seconds.
items.mistmeadow-buckler: Tanks stacking magical armor who can regenerate and block up to half incoming damage with their passive.
items.eviscerator: Attackers looking for a cheap item that boosts attack speed and lifesteal in short fights.
items.oathkeeper: Ability users who want to blend spellcasting with basic attack empowerment every 1.5 seconds.
items.mindrazor: Physical damage dealers dealing area damage around targets, gaining scaling from their own physical power.
items.exodus: Fighters needing crowd control and burst damage with a cheap foundational item for early game.
items.iceskorn-talons: Team fighters who want to boost allies' damage and slow enemies while standing near them.
items.crescelia: Casters who spam abilities want the 20% cooldown reduction and 45 magical power for constant offense.
items.tainted-totem: Support heroes buy this to cripple enemy healers with 45% healing reduction while gaining tankiness.
items.tainted-charm: Tanky supports use this to slow and weaken enemy healing by 45% while gaining 350 health and crowd control resistance.
items.devotion: Healers and shielders pick this for 10% stronger heals on low allies and damage reduction against big hits.
items.spellbreaker: Magic defense specialists want 75 magical power, a spell shield, and 40 magical armor to counter ability-heavy enemies.
items.rebirth: Divers and aggressive heroes buy this to survive fights and resurrect as a drone after 210 second cooldown.
items.hexbound-bracers: Mana-hungry fighters gain mana back from damage taken plus health and shield strength for sustained teamfights.
items.marshal: Basic attackers and ability users want 45 magical power, 15% attack speed, and bonus on-hit damage for steady output.
items.plasma-blade: Critical strikers stack up to 5 times for 20% critical chance, gaining 4% more per hit over 3 seconds.
items.pacifier: Low-health assassins use the 2.5x damage multiplier against targets below 20% health and recast on kills to finish.
items.basilisk: Melee fighters who stack attacks need armor shred against tanky enemies stacking physical armor.
items.cursed-scroll: Aggressive casters who can sustain themselves benefit from the attack speed and health regen boost.
items.salvation: Tanky heroes need the shield and tenacity to survive fights going long against burst damage.
items.volcanica: Ability-spamming heroes who cast ultimates frequently want the cooldown reduction stacking advantage.
items.tainted-scepter: Magic damage heroes needing healing reduction pick this for shutting down enemy sustain and scaling.
items.noxia: Casters who mark targets leverage the max health damage bonus to burst through tanks reliably.
items.syonic-echo: Physical attackers resetting basic attacks gain 30 percent attack speed to chain damage quickly.
items.titan-crest: All heroes start with this free item healing 340 health to evolve into stronger form.
items.spectral-schematics: Ability users with cooldown reduction deal 40 damage plus 5 percent current health scaling bonus.
items.megacosm: Mages who fight tanky enemies benefit from the 2.5% max health damage scaling and 65 magic power.
items.typhoon: Attack-speed builders stack attack speed quickly with 3% per hit, up to 4 stacks, plus 30 magic power.
items.imperator: Crit-focused physical damage dealers amplify their 20% crit chance with 30% more damage per critical strike.
items.luxforge-lantern: Support players gain 15 ability haste and 30 physical power while earning up to 30 extra power from takedowns.
items.fire-blossom: Tanky fighters with 350 health and 50 armor deal scaling damage starting at 15 per second, up to 50% more.
items.ortus: Physical damage heroes use 90-second cooldown burst for 20% speed and 10% power, stacking 3 power per takedown.
items.assassin-crest: Early-game physical assassins restore 3 health on hits and 4 on kills to evolve into a stronger legendary item.
items.entropy: Mages who spam abilities deal 3 base plus 3% bonus magic damage, once per ability per target every 0.2 seconds.
items.tainted-rounds: Physical attackers reduce enemy healing by 45% while dealing up to 100% more damage as targets lose health.
items.dynamo: Tanky ability users gain 20 physical power and 300 health while making enemies take 10% more damage for 2.5 seconds.
items.vyzar-carapace: Melee fighters who want to trigger shield procs repeatedly while building damage and tankiness.
items.voidgazer: Mages and ranged attackers who need to soften targets from range and reduce enemy speed.
items.void-helm: Tanks who rely on healing, since the 20% healing boost scales with all sources.
items.azure-core: Mages stacking ability power who farm camps constantly to unlock extra power at 400 mana.
items.gravitum: Supports stopping enemies in place with a ground effect while sustaining with lifesteal.
items.dust-devil: Attack-speed attackers stacking critical hits to unlock movement speed bonuses for dueling.
items.tainted-bastion: Frontliners punishing healing-heavy enemies while reducing their sustain by 45% for 3 seconds.
items.solstice: Bruisers farming damage and converting takedowns into burst healing via stacked damage scaling.
items.champion-crest: Early-game fighters self-healing in lane to trigger evolution into a stronger legendary crest.
items.aegis-of-agawar: Durable fighters gaining armor scaling and doubling defense when low, countering burst damage.
items.xenia: Supports protect allies with repeated shields, trading 2450 gold for constant 50-point defensive layers every 10 seconds.
items.soulbearer: Free mages gain 100-point shields, 20% healing from spells, and 10% speed when their 120-second ultimate hits.
items.cursed-ring: Attack-speed junkies trade 25% basic damage for 1.2x attack speed and 4 attacks per second, costing 3100 gold.
items.ashbringer: Crit-focused fighters reset ability cooldowns 8% per hit, doubled on crits, combining 45 power with 20% crit chance.
items.warp-stream: Mages and supports escape danger every 120 seconds via Tele-Blink and boost ultimate cooldowns 25% for 2200 gold.
items.tainted-trident: Physical dealers cut enemy healing by 45% and deal 3% more damage, enabling execution strategies for 2900 gold.
items.unbroken-will: Tanks absorb 25% more physical and magical damage for 2.5s with 400 health and 75 tenacity stacked.
items.leafsong: Free supports speed nearby allies 30% and block slows, granting a third ward slot and passive gold.
items.wizard-crest: Free mages farm mana restoration, evolving into Legendary Crest after restoring 300 mana total.
items.necrosis: Ultimate-reliant carries gain 25% ultimate haste and 15% ultimate damage, scaling 40 power and 20% crit.
items.spectra: Pick this for sustained auto-attack damage when you need cooldown reduction and on-hit procs against grouped enemies.
items.timewarp: Choose this when you spam abilities frequently, since it cuts 1s off non-ultimate cooldowns every time you cast.
items.echelon-cloak: Build this for hit-and-run gameplay—hide with camouflage, gain 8% damage boost and health regen for 3s after leaving.
items.elafrost: Take this for melee heroes who need slow control, with a 1.5s cooldown empowered attack dealing 70 damage around targets.
items.manta-scythe: Build this against isolated enemies since you deal 10% more damage to them and gain permanent power from siege kills.
items.infernum: Pick this to stack kindling fast and ignite enemies, dealing up to 4% of their max health over 2s.
items.malady: Choose this for scaling into late game where your takedown cooldown reset and stacking damage turn kills into streaks.
items.ruination: Build this to drain enemy mana while dealing true damage equal to 80% of what you drain, refreshing the bleed with abilities.
items.void-conduit: Take this cheap item for sustain and tankiness, gaining 6% omnivamp and mana regen when you're low on resources.
items.stonewall: Build this pure defensive item against physical teams, mitigating 5% of damage plus up to 70 armor and 80 health regen.
items.liberator: Support or tank who needs instant debuff removal and a quick shield to survive burst damage.
items.demon-edge: Physical damage dealer who wants to ignore armor and deal massive bonus damage for 2 seconds.
items.rogue-crest: Early-game assassin farming minions to heal 3 health per kill and evolve into a stronger item.
items.caustica: Magic damage dealer ignoring 35% enemy magic armor while gaining more damage the tankier they build.
items.orb-of-growth: Mage stacking experience from minions and fights to eventually evolve into a stronger form with healing.
items.mesmer: Physical attacker with 250 health who blocks one enemy ability every 60 seconds in fights.
items.sanctification: Support shielding nearby allies for 150 health plus giving them damage resistance for 3 seconds.
items.cybernetic-drive: Tank or bruiser executing low-health enemies while gaining 12% armor from both physical and magical sources.
items.warden-crest: Support generating free healing and gold every 30 seconds with a nearby ally present.
items.lightning-hawk: Attack-speed crit carry who slows enemies after stacking 100 attack-based momentum for burst damage.
items.resolution: Physical damage dealers who attack fast and need mana sustain to spam abilities repeatedly.
items.vanquisher: High-damage physical assassins finishing weakened enemies with execute mechanics instantly.
items.equinox: Melee fighters needing shields and tenacity to survive burst damage while staying mobile.
items.saphirs-mantle: Tanks who grow stronger by absorbing damage and converting it into permanent health.
items.alternator: Ability-focused physical heroes who trigger their alternate ability repeatedly for consistent damage.
items.windcaller: Supports sharing movement speed boosts with allies while providing healing and mana sustain.
items.judgement: Melee bruisers healing themselves by hitting multiple enemies with area damage repeatedly.
items.vanguardian: Balanced defenders resisting both physical and magical damage while reducing ability cooldowns.
items.guardian-crest: Supports generating gold passively while healing nearby allies every thirty seconds.
items.transference: Tanky supports converting absorbed shields into personal health for sustained teamfights.
items.scattershot: Ranged attackers who need consistent damage and critical strikes to burst targets from distance.
items.earth-spirit: Tanky initiators who want to stun enemies and reduce cooldowns by landing hero collisions.
items.tainted-guard: Bruisers facing healing-heavy enemies, using armor and bleed to reduce enemy sustain by 45%.
items.golems-gift: Ability-spamming tanks who gain armor and magic power by casting spells and losing stacks to damage.
items.goliath-crest: Any hero who wants passive healing and evolution, gaining stats after restoring 340 health over time.
items.soulbinder: Long-range magic dealers who deal 7% bonus damage at 1450+ range and permanently scale power.
items.tyranny: Fighters chasing kills, gaining movement speed, ability haste, and ultimate haste every 45 seconds.
items.winters-fury: Mages controlling fights with an expanding slow zone that makes enemies take 10% more magic damage.
items.frostguard: Tanks punishing attackers, stacking attack speed reduction up to 5 times every second nearby.
items.vainglory: Durable frontliners who stack armor bonuses up to 10 times while fighting, plus 10% flat increase.
items.world-breaker: Mages who need tankiness and stack ability rotations to scale their magical damage output over time.
items.wraith-leggings: Mages wanting raw magical power and penetration to ignore enemy defenses while abilities hit harder.
items.orion: Ability-focused heroes who convert cooldown reduction into attack speed and ramp damage against single targets.
items.epoch: Any hero needing a cheap mana and power base with an emergency escape button for tight spots.
items.dawnstar: Melee fighters who weave basic attacks to debilitate enemies and reset their burst defense repeatedly.
items.sky-splitter: Ranged attackers who convert sustained hits into percentage-based health damage and self-healing pressure.
items.oblivion-crown: Mages stacking magical power to multiply their scaling with that bonus multiplier into massive damage.
items.catalytic-drive: Hybrid heroes needing tankiness who execute low-health targets while farming gold and healing themselves.
items.painweaver: Physical damage dealers who stack their penetration and mobility through repeated attacks in extended fights.
items.the-perforator: Cooldown-heavy physical heroes ignoring armor while slowing enemies, turning ability spam into combat control.
items.nightfall: Physical damage dealers who sustain through fights with the 6% healing and need to break through 10 penetration.
items.augmentation: Attack-speed builders who want burst damage every 1.5s and benefit from 20 ability haste for frequent ability use.
items.warlock-crest: Support heroes who heal allies and want to evolve into a stronger legendary item after restoring 200 health.
items.envy: Physical damage dealers who need guaranteed crits every 2.5s and silence enemies for 0.8s with 45 power.
items.witchstalker: Fighters who cleanse debuffs every 165s and deal up to 8% of target max health while self-healing.
items.deathstalker: Attack-speed physical dealers who stack armor shred up to 4 times and double their attack speed for sustained damage.
items.alternata: Ability users who want 15% more alternate ability damage and 12% faster cooldowns on other basic abilities.
items.penumbra: Physical ability casters who reduce non-ultimate cooldowns by 4s and penetrate with 50 power and 20 haste.
items.pygmy-dust: Support heroes who shrink enemies for 3.5s, gain movement speed, and increase ward cap to 3.
items.legacy: Tankiness builders who need 75 tenacity and 3s cc immunity every 120s against hard crowd control.
items.fist-of-razuul: Melee fighters who want survivability and sustained damage, stacking empowered attacks that heal them 65% of bonus damage dealt.
items.sharpshooter-crest: Ranged attackers who sustain through fights, gaining lifesteal and attack speed to trigger evolution after restoring 150 health.
